Global (All).title: Global Supply Chain Disruptions due to Ukraine-Russia Conflict
Global (All).summary: The ongoing conflict between Ukraine and Russia has led to disruptions in global supply chains, with many countries experiencing delays and shortages in essential goods.

Global (All).title: Covid-19 Pandemic: Global Infection Rates Rise
Global (All).summary: The COVID-19 pandemic continues to spread globally, with infection rates rising in many countries, putting a strain on healthcare systems and economies.

Global (All).title: Global Economic Uncertainty due to Rising Inflation
Global (All).summary: Rising inflation rates in many countries are causing economic uncertainty, with fears of a global recession and potential impacts on consumer spending and business investments.

United States.title: Gun violence on the rise in US cities
United States.summary: Shootings and homicides have increased in several US cities, including Chicago, New York, and Los Angeles, raising concerns about gun control and public safety.

United States.title: Jan 6 investigation: House panel subpoenas Trump aides
United States.summary: The House committee investigating the January 6 US Capitol attack has subpoenaed several Trump aides, including former White House officials and Trump's personal lawyer.

United States.title: US-Mexico border tensions escalate
United States.summary: Tensions along the US-Mexico border have increased due to a surge in migrant crossings and a rise in cartel violence, prompting the US to deploy more troops and resources to the area.

South Korea.title: China's Xi Says Relations With North Korea Are Stable
South Korea.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping told South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol that relations between the two countries and North Korea are stable, in a phone call that marked the first conversation between the two leaders since Yoon took office in May.

South Korea.title: U.S. Ambassador to Seoul Says North Korea's Provocations Are Intentional
South Korea.summary: U.S. Ambassador to Seoul Philip Goldberg said North Korea's recent provocations, including a missile test earlier this month, were intentional and aimed at drawing attention from regional actors.

South Korea.title: South Korea, US Hold Joint Military Drills Amid North Korean Missile Launches
South Korea.summary: South Korea and the United States held joint military drills, which included a simulated attack on North Korea, amid a recent increase in missile launches by the North.

Japan.title: COVID-19 cases surge in Japan as omicron variant spreads
Japan.summary: Japan reported over 25,000 new COVID-19 cases over the past week, with the omicron variant spreading quickly across the country.

Japan.title: Japan's Prime Minister Fumio Kishida to dissolve parliament ahead of snap election
Japan.summary: Japan's Prime Minister Fumio Kishida announced on Monday that he will dissolve parliament to hold a snap election, citing a need for a new mandate to address the country's economic and social challenges.

Japan.title: Tokyo police deploy riot gear ahead of major Olympics event
Japan.summary: Tokyo police deployed riot gear and water cannons ahead of a major Olympics event, following concerns over extremist groups and protesters.
